properties.name: server.netty.websocket.cluster.enable
properties.description: Master switch for WebSocket cluster mode. Default false — single-node mode with zero cluster overhead. Only when true does the cluster auto-configuration activate.

properties.name: server.netty.websocket.cluster.node-id
properties.description: Unique identifier for this node. Null or empty auto-generates a UUID.

properties.name: server.netty.websocket.cluster.redis.uri
properties.description: Redis connection URI. Topology is selected by the scheme: redis://host:port (standalone), redis-sentinel://host:port/?sentinelMasterId=... (sentinel). Use rediss:// for TLS and redis://:password@host for auth — Redis is a trusted control plane and MUST be secured in production.

properties.name: server.netty.websocket.cluster.heartbeat-interval-seconds
properties.description: How often this node writes its heartbeat to Redis.

properties.name: server.netty.websocket.cluster.heartbeat-timeout-seconds
properties.description: How long before a missing heartbeat marks a node as dead.

properties.name: server.netty.websocket.cluster.reconciliation-interval-seconds
properties.description: Slow-path reconciliation sweep interval — backstop for missed keyspace notifications (fire-and-forget, no replay).

properties.name: server.netty.websocket.cluster.drain-timeout-seconds
properties.description: Max time to wait for sessions to close during graceful shutdown.

properties.name: server.netty.websocket.cluster.reconnect-jitter-max-seconds
properties.description: Max jitter applied before a DEGRADED to RESYNC re-registration, to avoid reconnect storms when many nodes recover simultaneously.

properties.name: server.netty.websocket.cluster.redis-loss-grace-period-ms
properties.description: Grace period (ms) before a transport loss degrades the node state machine (debounce). A blip shorter than this will not flap the node or trigger on-redis-loss=close-all. The broker's own state still flips immediately for truthful health. 0 = instant degrade (1.8.0 behavior).

properties.name: server.netty.websocket.cluster.on-redis-loss
properties.description: Behavior when the cluster transport (Redis) is lost. DEGRADE_TO_LOCAL (default) keeps local sessions alive and continues local fan-out, pausing only cross-node traffic. CLOSE_ALL closes all local sessions (fail-fast / load-shed).

properties.name: server.netty.websocket.cluster.on-publish-failure
properties.description: Behavior when a cluster publish/unicast fails or a message is too large. LOG (default) logs a warning; DROP silently drops (only the publishFailures counter increments).

properties.name: server.netty.websocket.cluster.registry-read-cache-ttl-ms
properties.description: Local sessionId-to-nodeId cache TTL (ms) for the unicast hot path.

properties.name: server.netty.websocket.cluster.command-timeout-ms
properties.description: Redis command timeout (ms) for the cluster control plane. Bounds how long any single Redis operation (including the unicast hot-path registry lookup) can block when Redis is unreachable — much lower than Lettuce's 60s default.

properties.name: server.netty.websocket.cluster.pubsub-connections
properties.description: Number of Redis Pub/Sub SUBSCRIBE connections to spread inbound decode across by channel hash. 1 (default) = single connection, unchanged. Use 2-4 only when a node approaches the single Lettuce pub/sub connection decode ceiling (~80k msg/s). Range [1,16]. Redis-Pub/Sub-specific.

properties.name: server.netty.websocket.cluster.nats.servers
properties.description: Comma-separated NATS server URLs (nats://host:port,...). When non-empty, the NatsClusterBroker replaces the Redis Pub/Sub broker (transport only; SessionRegistry and heartbeat stay on Redis). Requires io.nats:jnats on the classpath. Empty (default) = Redis broker.

properties.name: server.netty.websocket.cluster.nats.registry
properties.description: When true (and nats.servers is set), run the SessionRegistry/heartbeat/reaper on NATS JetStream KV instead of Redis - a fully NATS-only deployment (no Redis). Requires a JetStream-enabled NATS server (nats-server -js). Default false (mixed: NATS broker + Redis registry).

properties.name: server.netty.websocket.cluster.message-max-size-bytes
properties.description: Max serialized cluster message size in bytes. Larger messages are not published to the cluster (local delivery is unaffected); handled per on-publish-failure. 0 means unlimited.

properties.name: server.netty.websocket.cluster.session-registry-write-rate
properties.description: Max sustained session register+deregister writes per second per node. Absorbs reconnect storms by coalescing and throttling registry writes (never dropping a register). Under this rate, writes pass straight through with no added latency. 0 means unlimited (pure pass-through).

properties.name: server.netty.websocket.cluster.reliable.enable
properties.description: Opt-in at-least-once reliable broadcast over Redis Streams (reliableBroadcast()). Disabled by default — when false there are no consumer threads, no extra Redis connection, and reliableBroadcast() throws. Existing topicMessage() (Pub/Sub, at-most-once) is unaffected either way.

properties.name: server.netty.websocket.cluster.reliable.stream-max-len
properties.description: Per-URI Redis Stream MAXLEN (approximate) for reliable broadcast — the at-least-once retention window. A node offline longer than this many entries of history on a URI misses the trimmed ones (bounded gap).

properties.name: server.netty.websocket.cluster.reliable.poll-block-ms
properties.description: XREADGROUP BLOCK timeout (ms) for the reliable consume loop.

properties.name: server.netty.websocket.cluster.reliable.poll-count
properties.description: XREADGROUP COUNT (max entries per read) for the reliable consume loop.

properties.name: server.netty.websocket.cluster.reliable.dedup-window
properties.description: Per-URI ring size of recently-acked stream entry ids, to de-duplicate in-process redelivery (at-least-once still allows cross-crash duplicates; handlers should be idempotent).

properties.name: server.netty.websocket.cluster.auth.enable
properties.description: Opt-in HMAC authentication of cross-node envelopes (anti-forgery). Disabled by default. When true, every envelope is signed (HMAC-SHA256) and inbound envelopes with a missing/invalid tag are rejected — closing the threat where a Redis writer forges originNodeId / injects / force-closes sessions. Enable cluster-wide (shared secret); see the rolling-upgrade note in the release notes.

properties.name: server.netty.websocket.cluster.auth.secret
properties.description: Shared HMAC secret (UTF-8). REQUIRED when auth.enable=true. Externalize via an environment variable or secret manager (e.g. ${CLUSTER_AUTH_SECRET}); never hardcode. Use >= 32 chars. Redacted in logs.

properties.name: server.netty.websocket.cluster.auth.permissive
properties.description: Migration mode: when true, UNSIGNED inbound envelopes are accepted (while still signing outbound) so HMAC can be rolled out node-by-node without cross-node message loss; flip to false (strict) once all nodes sign. Default false.

values.description: Keep local sessions alive, continue local fan-out, pause cross-node traffic (default).
Close all local sessions when the transport is lost.
Log a warning on publish failure (default).
